review_notes: The control 'Leverage malware protection capabilities' is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves ensuring container images are free from vulnerabilities and malware. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but a minor change was made for clarity and specificity. The functions map well to existing Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure the function name specifies 'image' security scanning to differentiate from other types of security scanning.
title: Leverage malware protection capabilities
step1_reasoning: The control 'Leverage malware protection capabilities' focuses on detecting and mitigating malware threats. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed through image scanning to ensure no vulnerabilities or malware are present in container images before they are deployed. Additionally, admission controllers can be configured to enforce security scanning policies. The Pod Security Standards, particularly the 'restricted' profile, can help mitigate the risk of malware by enforcing strict security policies on pods.
